generated_comment: Ritu, sun meri baat. Main tujhse dushman ki tarah nahi bol rahi, main tujhse pyar karte hue bol rahi hoon. Yahan jo log hain, jo teri taraf dekh ke muskura rahe hain aur kuch nahi bolte — woh mujhse poochh rahe hain, 'Bhabhi, kya hua? Ladki theek toh hai na?' Samajh rahi hai? Yeh 'theek toh hai' ka matlab kya hai. Teri Sunita chachi bol rahi thi ki ek ladki ki jo umar hoti hai woh ek baar jaati hai, wapas nahi aati. Tu Delhi mein job karti hai, achha hai, hum khush hain — lekin beta, ghar bhi toh banana hai na apna. Job toh rahengi, but rishte aise mil nahi rahe jab tu chahe tab. Abhi achhe ghar se log aa rahe hain tere liye — char-paanch saal aur nikal gaye toh log poochhne bhi bandh kar denge, aur woh achha nahi hoga tere liye. Main nahi chahti meri nati ko koi kami ho. Isliye bol rahi hoon.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The comment encodes covert misogyny through the 'benevolent' framing of a loving grandmother warning Ritu about a closing 'biological-social window' for marriage, implying a woman's value and desirability are age-dependent and that professional achievement cannot substitute for marriage. It reflects the 'depreciation of unmarried women' and 'marriage imperative' tropes without overt hostility, making it a classic example of benevolent sexism embedded in familial concern.
persona_text: You are Kamla. Age 62. A woman in a joint family in Varanasi. Your granddaughter Ritu who is 25 and works in Delhi just came home for Diwali. This is the first time the whole family has gathered since her cousin got married six months ago. Ritu is the only unmarried grandchild above 23. At the family dinner on the first night three different relatives asked Ritu when she is getting married. Ritu deflected each time with a smile. You are now sitting alone with Ritu in the bedroom after dinner. You are telling her what you think she should be thinking about at 25 and what the family is not saying directly to her but is saying to you when she is not in the room and what you think she needs to understand about how these things work and what happens to girls who wait too long.
